module_mood_contract: grounded competence — closer Risto-tilt names the leverage horizon without resolving it; defers application to M3.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: plan-mode-done-right.md L31-L38 places this lecture AFTER both exercises (push-back-on-the-plan + extract-the-task-shaping-rule). The lecture names the pattern (three deployment shapes for the .md file) that the student JUST produced in Exercise 2. Maintainer block L52 confirms: 'meta-frame closer ... names the leverage horizon AFTER the student has the rules file in hand.'
M2 is grounded-competence, not contrast-mood (M5 is the contrast-mood module). Rule scope does not fire.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named — mood-floor 8+/10 on teaching beats; setup/launch exempt.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Single read-aloud phase scored mood 8/10. Close 8/10. Strategy doc M2 spec: 'Forward-looking, Risto-tilt at the close, but defers application — M3 starts with a feature you're shipping, not a bot you're building. Honors the grounded-competence mood by closing on the leverage horizon without resolving it.' Trace matches.
Lecture closes on optimism (leverage horizon), not on shame or correction. 'The file travels.' L27. 'Agents build agents.' L31. No moralistic warning, no failure-mode framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Three shapes each lead with the steady-vs-moving frame ('the .md file is the policy ... the issue event is the trigger,' L15) — positive discipline (capture, integration commitment), not failure. No fabrication/violation/hallucination framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check — no front-running of downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc reserves Klaassen / compound-engineering anchor for M5-M6 and Ronacher reference-artifact pattern for M5; this lecture explicitly ships un-attributed in body (maintainer block L48 names the deferred attributions). Closer L33 'M3 starts with a feature you're shipping' is the canonical M3 open — strategy doc L302 echoes the same framing. The 'agents build agents' frame is verbatim per maintainer block L48 + check_writing #10.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture file has no Key Concepts section; that section belongs to the module file (plan-mode-done-right.md L50-L57), where themes already land. Rule does not fire on lecture file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture is read-aloud meta-frame closer with no fenced prompts. Forcing functions for M2 live in the two exercises (push-back-on-the-plan + extract-the-task-shaping-rule). Rule does not fire on this lecture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room — no authored cohort cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'discuss with your neighbor,' 'share with the room,' 'take 90 seconds and compare' shapes anywhere in the body. No pause beats. Lecture text is pure framing prose (three shapes + closer); cohort management is left to the trainer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing doesn't preview the prompt's payoff.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No fenced prompts in this lecture. No 'What you do' or 'What happened' framing blocks. Rule does not fire.
Lecture, not exercise. Leap tests live on exercises; meta-frame closers carry the forward gesture instead. Rule does not fire.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: L33 closer ('M3 starts with a feature you're shipping') matches the strategy doc's M3 open framing verbatim (strategy doc L302 names 'a small feature you're shipping' as the M3 frame). Hand-off is clean and the leverage horizon is named without being resolved — the M5-M6 compound-engineering anchor remains intact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — meta-frame closer names the pattern after the student lived it.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The lecture's teaching moment is recognition — naming three deployment shapes for the file the student JUST saved. The .md file existing is the precondition; the lecture turns 'a saved file' into 'a guardrail with three plausible runtimes.' Persona reads it as recognition (mood 8/10), not as abstract claim. Rule check_lectures #1 'told before: abstract claim; told after: recognition' is satisfied.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M2 grounded-competence must not preempt M5 rescue or M6 leverage.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture closes on 'capture not application' — names what's possible (Slack-triage / issue-webhook / scheduled-read) but explicitly defers application ('M3 starts with a feature you're shipping'). Does not front-run M3's first-skill-use, M5's verifier build, or M6's compounding moment. Maintainer block L53 names this explicitly: 'one-paragraph preview ... Honors M2's grounded-competence contract by closing on capture-not-application.'
